# Feature: Protocol Types & Core Data Model

## 1. Feature Goal

**ID**: FEAT-001  
**Source Plan**: `.spec-lite/plan_websocket_sse.md`

Introduce protocol discrimination and core data model changes to support HTTP, WebSocket (WS), and Server-Sent Events (SSE) as first-class request types in Wave Client. This enables the UI, adapters, and persistence layers to handle all three protocols, laying the foundation for realtime features and protocol-specific UI/logic. Backward compatibility for existing HTTP collections is preserved.

## 2. Data Model (Granular)

### Entities & Attributes

- **CollectionRequest** (existing, extended):
  - `id` (string) — Unique identifier
  - `name` (string) — Display name
  - `protocol?` ('http') — Optional protocol discriminant (default 'http')
  - `method` (string) — HTTP method
  - `url` (CollectionUrl | string) — Request URL
  - `query?` (ParamRow[])
  - `header?` (HeaderRow[])
  - `body?` (CollectionBody)
  - `description?` (string)
  - `validation?` (RequestValidation)
  - `authId?` (string)
  - `sourceRef?` (CollectionReference)

- **WsCollectionRequest** (new):
  - `id` (string)
  - `name` (string)
  - `protocol` ('ws')
  - `url` (CollectionUrl | string)
  - `header?` (HeaderRow[])
  - `query?` (ParamRow[])
  - `description?` (string)
  - `authId?` (string)
  - `sourceRef?` (CollectionReference)

- **SseCollectionRequest** (new):
  - `id` (string)
  - `name` (string)
  - `protocol` ('sse')
  - `method` (string)
  - `url` (CollectionUrl | string)
  - `header?` (HeaderRow[])
  - `query?` (ParamRow[])
  - `body?` (CollectionBody)
  - `description?` (string)
  - `authId?` (string)
  - `sourceRef?` (CollectionReference)

- **AnyCollectionRequest** (new): Discriminated union of the above three types.

- **Type Guards** (new):
  - `isHttpRequest(req): req is CollectionRequest`
  - `isWsRequest(req): req is WsCollectionRequest`
  - `isSseRequest(req): req is SseCollectionRequest`
  - `getRequestProtocol(req): RequestProtocol`

- **Enums/Types**:
  - `type RequestProtocol = 'http' | 'ws' | 'sse'`

### Relationships

- `CollectionItem.request` → `AnyCollectionRequest` (1:1)
- `Collection` → `CollectionItem[]` (existing)

### Indexes & Constraints

- No new DB indexes (in-memory/JSON storage). Protocol field is required for WS/SSE, optional for HTTP.

## 3. Files

- `packages/core/src/types/collection.ts` — Extend `CollectionRequest`, add `WsCollectionRequest`, `SseCollectionRequest`, `AnyCollectionRequest`, type guards, and protocol enum.
- `packages/core/src/types/adapters.ts` — Update adapter interfaces to accept `AnyCollectionRequest` where appropriate (future-proofing, if needed).
- `packages/core/src/utils/requestTypeGuards.ts` (new) — Implement type guard functions for protocol discrimination.
- `packages/core/src/test/types/collection.test.ts` — Unit tests for type guards and backward compatibility.
- `packages/core/src/test/types/requestTypeGuards.test.ts` — Unit tests for type guards (if not colocated).

## 4. Dependencies

- None (first in dependency chain)

## 5. Implementation Tasks

### TASK-001: Extend CollectionRequest and Add Protocol Types

- [ ] **Implementation**: 
  - Add optional `protocol?: 'http'` to `CollectionRequest` (default to 'http' if missing).
  - Define `WsCollectionRequest` and `SseCollectionRequest` types as per plan.
  - Define `AnyCollectionRequest` as a discriminated union.
  - Add `RequestProtocol` enum/type.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: 
  - Test serialization/deserialization of all three request types.
  - Test backward compatibility (old collections load as HTTP).
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: 
  - Update JSDoc/type comments in `collection.ts`.
- **Verify**: 
  - All request types are serializable, type guards work, and old collections load as HTTP.

### TASK-002: Implement Type Guards for Protocol Discrimination

- [ ] **Implementation**: 
  - Implement `isHttpRequest`, `isWsRequest`, `isSseRequest`, `getRequestProtocol` in `requestTypeGuards.ts` (or colocated if preferred).
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: 
  - Test all type guards with valid and invalid inputs.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: 
  - Add/extend JSDoc for all type guards.
- **Verify**: 
  - Type guards return correct types for all cases, including missing protocol (should default to HTTP).

### TASK-003: Update CollectionItem and Related Types to Use AnyCollectionRequest

- [ ] **Implementation**: 
  - Update `CollectionItem.request` to accept `AnyCollectionRequest`.
  - Update usages in `collection.ts` and any related types.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: 
  - Test that collections with WS/SSE requests are valid and serializable.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: 
  - Update type comments and any relevant docs.
- **Verify**: 
  - CollectionItem can hold any protocol request and round-trips through serialization.

### TASK-004: Add/Update Tests for Backward Compatibility

- [ ] **Implementation**: 
  - Add tests that load legacy collections (no protocol field) and verify they are treated as HTTP.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: 
  - See above (test cases for legacy data).
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: 
  - Document backward compatibility behavior in test and type docs.
- **Verify**: 
  - Legacy collections load and function as HTTP requests.

## 6. Cross-Cutting Concerns

- **Auth**: No change; WS/SSE requests may reference `authId` for future use.
- **Error Handling**: Type guards and deserialization must not throw; return safe defaults.
- **Logging**: N/A for type/model changes.
